title: CP0 Comes to Egghead
description: On pages 7–9, this part focuses on Unknown person #7, Law, Kizaru, Vegapunk, Unknown person #8, Luffy as Vegapunk’s satellites reveal the island’s technology and ancient past while CP0 approaches and Bonney pursues the truth about Kuma.
scene: Vegapunk’s satellites reveal the island’s technology and ancient past while CP0 approaches and Bonney pursues the truth about Kuma.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Black vortex!

dialogue.speaker: Law
dialogue.text: Blackbeard and TraFAlgar Law...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Have made contact on winner Island in the new world!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: So we're forced to wait again...

dialogue.speaker: Kizaru
dialogue.text: Being fleet Admiral...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: ...Is so damn frustrating!!

dialogue.speaker: Vegapunk
dialogue.text: Meanwhile, on egghead,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: The island of the future

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Wow, the Pacific stopped as soon as we changed....!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's good, but did you have to make me this old, Bonney?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: And I get how you feel, but...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: whereze, where

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: it only looks like your dad. If we can't take any of 'em out, we'll be in trouble--

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Have a heart, Luffy! Sorry, Bonney. Pacificas just bring up some bad memories for us.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Kuma may no longer be human, but that doesn't make it any easier for his daughter to come face to face with his doppelganger...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: "Hey, can you turn me back...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: PANT... PAINT...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Nothing personal, Bonney...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But what I know about Kuma, doesn't paint a pretty picture.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: He was the TVRAMNICAL King of sorbet...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Before he was deposed by the people and forced to become a pirate.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: When he was eventually caught by the Navy,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: His ties to the revolutionaries came to light...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: And they gave him a life sentence.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Vegapunk took an interest in him due to his physical prowess,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So he was offered the opportunity to roam the seas again as a warlord...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: on the condition that he become a specimen...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: ...for body modification and cloning!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Does that cover it?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I get why he'd choose to be a warlord dog and help them make their clone soldiers...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But why would anyone knowingly agree to become a mindless cyborg...?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Anyone can see it's just as bad as a death sentence!!

summary: This part advances the Egghead / Vegapunk Truth through 32 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
